summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Payroll Canada MVP - Backend API issues with pay period creation due to MongoDB ObjectId serialization, but frontend and most backend endpoints working correctly

critical_bugs.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error due to MongoDB ObjectId serialization problem
critical_bugs.impact: Blocks pay period creation and downstream payroll processing workflow
critical_bugs.error_details: ValueError: [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')]
critical_bugs.root_cause: FastAPI trying to serialize MongoDB ObjectId in JSON response

working_endpoints.status: Working - Mock timesheet preview with 75 entries

design_issues.issue: Modal overlay blocking navigation after pay period form opens

passed_tests: Dashboard stats display with employee count and pay periods
Timesheet import preview (75 matched entries from mock data)
Frontend navigation between all major pages
Professional UI design with Any Minute branding
Mock timesheet connector generating realistic data
Employee data display with rates and employment types

success_percentage.backend: 70% - Most endpoints working, critical issue with pay period creation
success_percentage.frontend: 95% - Excellent UI/UX, minor modal overlay issue

action_item_for_main_agent.critical_fix_required: Fix MongoDB ObjectId serialization issue in pay period creation endpoint (/api/pay-periods). This is blocking the core payroll workflow. Consider using UUID instead of ObjectId or implement proper JSON serialization for ObjectId.
action_item_for_main_agent.investigation_needed: Review all database operations to ensure consistent use of UUIDs vs ObjectIds to prevent similar serialization issues in other endpoints
action_item_for_main_agent.testing_note: Once pay period creation is fixed, the full payroll workflow (create period → import timesheets → calculate payroll → generate payslips) should be retested
